macro_summary: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). Thursday's session saw equities rebound (SPY +0.85%, QQQ +1.54%) as oil prices fell 2.6% to $72.34 on nascent US-Iran peace hopes, even as military exchanges continued. Semis led with SOXX +2.98%, while the 10-year yield dipped to 4.54%. Volatility eased, with VIX dropping 6% to 15.84.
commodities_note: WTI crude eased to $72.34 from Wednesday's $74.57 spike as markets weighed both renewed US-Iran negotiations and ongoing shipping disruptions. Gold bounced 1.6% to $4,137, and the dollar index slipped 0.4%.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain severely extended (RSI ≥ 80) with no NIM relief in sight. US10Y at 4.54% stays above the 4.20% threshold for funding-cost relief, and MAS has not eased the S$NEER policy. No entry until a pullback or a rate catalyst.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bullish — NIM recovery on hold; awaiting rate catalyst.
singapore.key_development: None; the cluster continues to drift higher on momentum, far above re-entry zones.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster rallied sharply (SOXX +2.98%) led by AMD (+5.8%) on a Morgan Stanley supply chain report, but the move was inconsistent — NVDA slipped, and many names remain extended. SK Hynix's $28B US listing (7x oversubscribed, per Reuters) underscores AI memory demand, but Taiwan's central bank AI bubble warning and Intel's extreme 200-day extension flag speculative excess within the group.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish but valuation stretch and concentration risk are rising — six semis names are hard-blocked on extension.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD rallied on supply chain optimism despite Meta's in-house AI chip plans threatening medium-term demand; SK Hynix listing demand confirms HBM appetite.

big_tech.summary: BigTech holds steady as earnings season approaches. Amazon remains HOLD with AI capex overhang, while Microsoft's HOLD reflects easing OpenAI dependency as GPT-5.6 is reaffirmed for Copilot. Google's pre-earnings band suggests a binary on July 22 with Cloud revenue as the focal point.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but revenue payoff and AI competition remain unproven.
big_tech.key_development: OpenAI designating GPT-5.6 as the preferred model for Microsoft Copilot (per TechCrunch) reduces fears of an imminent breakup, but Azure's non-OpenAI RPO remains the cleaner demand signal.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B strategic photonics investment (partner alongside LITE) — direct exposure to AI datacenter optics demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8–10% from current levels after LITE's NVDA deal-related bounce fades.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and optical transceiver partner for hyperscalers; recently shipped 5M+ photonic ICs with Tower Semiconductor (per Insider Monkey, July 9).
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor after TSMC earnings on July 16 for CoWoS allocation signals that affect Marvell's AI ASIC ramp.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI networking switch leader, mentioned as a Broadcom partner; directly benefits from hyperscaler datacenter buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if ANET pulls back to its 200-day moving average after the current AI networking rally cools.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Key HBM and memory competitor to SK Hynix and Micron; its HBM3E qualification struggles determine the memory duopoly's tightness.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for Samsung's HBM yield improvement updates; a breakthrough would compress HBM ASPs and pressure MU/SK Hynix.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz conflict: peace talks restart despite ongoing military strikes; tanker traffic near standstill (per Reuters, July 9).
US-China tech tensions: bipartisan EV ban bill advances; China weaponizes fertilizer and rare earth exports; critical mineral trade truce fragile (per Forbes/CleanTechnica).
Fed policy division: minutes show split on inflation outlook; Warsh's first Fed decision exposes uncertainty; CPI on July 14 pivotal.
AI bubble warnings: Taiwan central bank chief cautions on speculative capex financed by aggressive borrowing (per Reuters, July 9).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil eased to $72.34 on peace hopes, relieving pressure on equities and allowing semis to rally. Singapore banks remain extended with no rate catalyst; BigTech holds steady as earnings approach. VIX at 15.84 signals calm, but binaries from CPI/earnings next week keep entries limited.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US-Iran peace negotiations resumed (per Reuters), causing oil to fall 2.6% despite continued military exchanges.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate persists with sporadic attacks and negotiations; WTI ranges $70–75. Semis consolidate, BigTech holds before earnings. Singapore banks drift higher on momentum but no entry.

optimistic.description: US-Iran peace deal reopens Strait of Hormuz by end of July; WTI falls below $65, lifting tech and easing rate-hike fears. Semis extend rally, TSM and ASML earnings become positive catalysts.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz conflict escalates, WTI surges above $85; semis sell off as AI capex fears return. Singapore banks tumble on rate-hike shock. CPI comes in hot, Fed resumes hawkish stance.

wildcard.description: China EV ban or rare earth export controls trigger a broad trade war, gutting semis and disrupting global supply chains. All equity correlations rise to 1, VIX spikes.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold current positions; no new entries ahead of CPI and key semis earnings. Wait for pullbacks in extended Singapore banks and semis.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously opportunistic — no consensus entry; waiting for pullbacks or earnings clarity
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 6 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, concentrated in Singapore banks and extended semis/memory plays. VIX at 15.84 keeps risk gauges calm, but binary events from next week's CPI and key semis earnings limit new entries.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
